title: "Aliens Live Among Us...I've Met Them!" -Whitley Strieber Tells All
summary: Whitley Strieber discusses his famous 1985 abduction experience documented in 'Communion', childhood experiments possibly connected to Operation Paperclip scientists, his ongoing communication with non-human intelligence, and theories about why disclosure has been delayed including John von Neumann's concept that these beings must be 'believed into existence' to enter our reality.

acronyms.definition: Term used to describe intelligent beings that are not human

events.description: Roswell crash debris and bodies brought to Air Material Command where General Exon reportedly held one of the bodies
Whitley Strieber as a 7-year-old underwent experiments involving a Skinner box at Randolph Air Force Base
Robert Sarbacher allegedly set up UFO secrecy along with Oppenheimer according to David Grusch
Whitley Strieber's famous abduction experience that led to the book Communion

key_claims.claim: Robert Sarbacher died the night Whitley Strieber sent him a detailed account of his experience via UPS
General Exon held one of the bodies from the Roswell crash and said it was like a big insect
John von Neumann wrote a paper theorizing that non-human intelligence must be believed into existence to become part of our reality through affecting wave function collapse
The visitors chose Strieber specifically because he lacked scientific authority, allowing people to opt out of believing if they wanted to
Operation Paperclip scientists apparently lived in Strieber's childhood neighborhood near Randolph Air Force Base
Sarbacher was scrubbed from the internet and very little information about him remains available
Strieber has something in his body he believes is an implant that can be felt and appears on CT scans
The visitors are concerned about cultural colonization and don't want to overwhelm humanity or create slaves
Childhood experiments at age 7 involving a Skinner box destroyed Strieber's immune system
Ross Coulthart said Robert Sarbacher is the one person who could lay out the architecture of the UFO program
